---
title: "About Me"
format:
  html:
    theme: minty
    toc: false
---

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](ImNatalie.jpg){width=100% style="padding-right:20px;"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
Hi! I’m Natalie Torres, a math major at Cal State Fullerton graduating in Spring 2026. I’m especially interested in data science, where I enjoy working with messy data, identifying patterns, and turning analyses into clear, meaningful visualizations.

I’m part of Project EAGER, where I study assisted gene flow in selfing plant populations using statistical modeling and simulation in R. My work focuses on modeling how introducing genetic variation can influence population fitness over time.

I’m building this portfolio to share what I’ve learned, what I’m working on, and where I’m hoping to grow next!
:::
:::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Find Me Online

-   GitHub: [\@Nat-Tor](https://github.com/Nat-Tor)\
-   LinkedIn: [Natalie Torres](https://www.linkedin.com/in/natalietorres-o7/)\
-   Email: natalietor\@csu.fullerton.edu

> This portfolio is a work in progress so things are a little messy but more projects, updates, and results will be added over time. \<3
